_purpose: Courthouse Atlas for Mercer County, KY. Formed 1786 from Lincoln County. Home of Harrodsburg — the oldest permanent settlement in Kentucky (1774). Records begin from formation. No major courthouse fire. Shaker Village of Pleasant Hill records are a unique supplementary source.

previous_courthouses.location: Harrodsburg, early courthouses near Fort Harrod site

previous_courthouses.fate: Replaced by current building. No major fire.

record_losses.description: Mercer County has no record of a major courthouse fire. Records run continuously from the 1786 formation. As one of the earliest formed Kentucky counties, Mercer's records are among the oldest in the state.
record_losses.narrative_impact: If {ancestor_name} was in Mercer County after 1786, the records almost certainly survive. For pre-1786 records, check Lincoln County (the parent county).
record_losses.source: Kentucky Department for Libraries and Archives. Guide to Kentucky County Court Records.

wills_estates.gaps: Minor gaps in early loose papers.

vital_records.repository: Kentucky Cabinet for Health and Family Services; KDLA

substitute_sources.source_type: Shaker Village of Pleasant Hill records
substitute_sources.description: The Shakers maintained meticulous records — membership rolls, journals, financial ledgers, and correspondence. These records document Shaker members (many originally from Mercer County families) and the community's interactions with its neighbors.
substitute_sources.repository: Shaker Village of Pleasant Hill archives; Western Reserve Historical Society (Cleveland, OH); Filson Historical Society
substitute_sources.genealogical_value: Identifies individuals who joined the Shaker community. The Shakers' detailed record-keeping means that Shaker members are often better documented than their neighbors. The records also document hired laborers — including enslaved workers — who worked at the village.
substitute_sources.source: Thomas and Thomas, The Simple Spirit (1973).

substitute_sources.description: Mercer County was formed from Lincoln County. Pre-1786 records, including Fort Harrod-era documents, are in Lincoln County.
substitute_sources.repository: Lincoln County Clerk, Stanford, KY; KDLA
substitute_sources.genealogical_value: Essential for connecting pre-formation families. Fort Harrod settlers' earliest records are under Lincoln County.

narrative_hooks: Mercer County's records go back to 1786 — among the oldest in Kentucky. If {ancestor_name} was at Fort Harrod, the earliest records are under Lincoln County, the parent.

The Shaker Village of Pleasant Hill kept records that shame most county courthouses — if {ancestor_name} joined the Shakers or worked for them, the village archives at Pleasant Hill and the Western Reserve Historical Society document {pronoun_object} in extraordinary detail.
Mercer County's courthouse survived without fire or disaster — {ancestor_name}'s paper trail is clean if it exists.
Before 1786, this was Lincoln County — Fort Harrod's people are in the Lincoln County records at KDLA, not the Mercer County courthouse.
source_references: Kentucky Department for Libraries and Archives. Guide to Kentucky County Court Records.
Thomas, Samuel W., and James C. Thomas. The Simple Spirit. Harrodsburg, KY: Pleasant Hill Press, 1973.
Clark, Thomas D. A History of Kentucky. Lexington: John Bradford Press, 1960.
